refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall provide worksite area analysis including terrain representation.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR combined multiple obligations: terrain, obstacles, and work-zone representation. To ensure atomicity and clarity, it is split into separate FRs each addressing a single aspect of worksite area analysis. This avoids conflating different functional requirements and maintains solution neutrality by focusing on what the system must do, not how.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verify that the system can generate and provide a representation of the terrain of the worksite area independently from other features.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall provide worksite area analysis including terrain representation.
The NGHE solution shall provide worksite area analysis including obstacle representation.
The NGHE solution shall provide worksite area analysis including work-zone representation.

candidate_dps: Generate a digital terrain model representing the worksite topography
Create a semantic map encoding key site features relevant to planning and safety
Produce a unified worksite model integrating terrain and feature data for use by downstream functions
Provide separate data layers for terrain and site features accessible via a common interface
selected_dp.selected_dp: Produce a unified worksite model integrating terrain and feature data for use by downstream functions

potential_sources: Integration of terrain and feature data into a single model may create dependencies between data sources
coupling_check.notes: While integrating terrain and site features into a unified model introduces some coupling, it supports consistency and ease of access for planning and execution functions, aligning with acceptance criteria.

assumptions_risks: Assumes availability of accurate terrain and site feature data sources
Risk that integration complexity may delay model availability
Assumes that the unified model format is compatible with planning and execution modules
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Clarify the scope of site features to be included and specify interface requirements for the unified model to ensure modularity and extensibility.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module encompasses the core data processing and integration functions, including the unification of terrain and feature data into a comprehensive worksite model for downstream use.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives raw terrain and feature data streams from sensors and external sources.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Sends processed worksite model data to downstream functions for task execution.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Facilitates data exchange with other system components and external systems as needed.

module_containment_scores.notes: Highly cohesive as the DP is central to data integration and modeling, which is core to NGHE Solution. Minimal diffusion as most processing is internal.
Vehicle module primarily handles physical control and sensing; integrating data model is peripheral and would cause significant diffusion.
Teleoperation focuses on remote control and monitoring; integrating terrain and feature data is not central and would cause diffusion.
Safety Services focus on alerts and safety monitoring; data integration is outside its scope, leading to poor containment and high diffusion.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most appropriate boundary for containing the DP because it directly manages data integration and modeling functions, ensuring high cohesion and low diffusion.
containment_risks: Potential interface complexity with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules if data formats or update rates are not well standardized.
Risk of overloading NGHE Solution with too many responsibilities if other data processing tasks are added without clear separation.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP directly addresses the FR by specifying the production of a unified worksite model integrating terrain and feature data, which fulfills the requirement to provide worksite area analysis including terrain representation.

diffusion_risks: Minimal diffusion risk as the DP is highly contained within the NGHE Solution module, which centralizes data integration and modeling functions.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module shows high cohesion with a containment score of 9 and low diffusion score of 2, indicating effective containment of the DP. Other modules have low containment and high diffusion scores, supporting the choice of NGHE Solution as the implementation boundary.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible as the NGHE Solution module outputs the unified worksite model to downstream planning and execution functions, enabling validation against acceptance criteria for model availability and content.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of responsibilities is established, with the NGHE Solution module owning the core data integration and modeling, and external interfaces defined for data input and output.

recommended_revisions: Establish standardized data formats and update rates for interfaces with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules to mitigate interface complexity risks.
Monitor the scope of NGHE Solution to avoid overloading with additional responsibilities; consider modularizing future data processing tasks to maintain cohesion.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed with strong justification and minimal diffusion risks. The NGHE Solution module effectively contains the DP with high cohesion and low diffusion, and verification feasibility and allocation clarity are established. Given the high safety criticality, it is prudent to maintain the current level of decomposition to ensure stability and focus on implementing recommended interface standardizations and monitoring module scope rather than further decomposing at this stage.

revise.why: Establish standardized data formats and update rates for interfaces with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules to mitigate interface complexity risks, and monitor the scope of the NGHE Solution module to avoid overloading and maintain cohesion.

assumptions: The NGHE Solution module will remain the central implementation boundary for the unified worksite model.
Interface standardization will reduce complexity and potential integration errors.
open_risks: Potential interface complexity with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules if data formats and update rates are not standardized.
Risk of NGHE Solution module becoming overloaded if additional responsibilities are added without modularization.
evidence_needed: Verification results confirming the unified worksite model meets acceptance criteria.
Metrics on cohesion and diffusion scores after interface standardization and scope monitoring.
